description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages test, publish, deploy, and publish-docker. In the test stage add a unit-test-job that uses the maven:3.3.9-jdk-8 Docker image, runs `mvn package -B`, and stores the generated `webapp/target/*.war` files as artifacts that expire in 5 days. In the publish-docker stage add a docker-build job that runs on the docker:latest image with the docker:dind service, logs in to the GitLab Container Registry using `$CI_REGISTRY_USER` and `$CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD`, builds the image with `docker build --pull -t "$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG" .` and pushes it, and is executed for all branches except master. Also in the publish-docker stage add a docker-build-master job that uses the same image, logs in similarly, builds the image with `docker build --pull -t "$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE" .` and pushes it, and runs only on the master branch. In the deploy stage add a publish-job that uses the maven:3-jdk-8 image, installs ssh-agent and rsync, starts the ssh agent, renames `webapp/target/lh-toolkit.war` to `master.war`, adds the `$SSH_PRIVATE_KEY`, configures SSH to skip host checking, and rsyncs the WAR to `ci@toolkit.librehealth.io:/var/lib/tomcat9/webapps`.
